rows.source_ref: wilczasty, 'Silly Scalping EA', Babypips forum thread (2021-2023), https://forums.babypips.com/t/silly-scalping-ea/560458
rows.concept: Fade a candle whose body exceeds an ATR multiple, then average down (each candle or on an ATR-spaced grid) until aggregate unrealized P/L hits a small fixed profit target, with no stop loss.
rows.eligibility_reason: Averaging-down / grid martingale with no stop loss - incompatible with FTMO daily-loss/max-DD and DXZ 5%/20% DD; author warns profitability depends on unlimited position-adding and it 'can eat your margin like CRAZY'.
rows.notes: Three variants of one core money-management engine, so one row: (v1) per-candle averaging with 3-pip TP, H1 GBPUSD; (v2) ATR*integer-spaced grid to an aggregate profit target, targets 10-20 open levels, M15 EURUSD/GBPUSD tested with up to 95 open trades; (v3) RSI-triggered entry (#27), same open-until-aggregate-profit engine, ~1% p.a. per pair at 1:30 'safest' settings. Salvageable kernel is only the candle-body>ATR*k mean-reversion entry, but it would need a hard stop + fixed risk (never specified in the thread) to become ELIGIBLE. Author self-describes the method as 'gambling'/'silly'; backtests are unverified forum screenshots (grand claim 25%/1900 trades later walked back to 10-15%).
